item.title: Up 75% Already in 2026, Korea’s Stock Market Is Hotter Than Ever
item.description: South Korean stocks are on the cusp of surpassing last year’s world-beating 76% gain, even though 2026 is less than five months old.

item.title: Egypt Opens High-Speed Monorail Linking Cairo to Desert Capital
item.description: Egypt opened the first phase of a showpiece monorail project, part of a $4.5 billion plan to connect Cairo with its satellite cities and a new capital in the desert.

item.title: Dollar Hits Lowest Since War Started on Report End Is Near
item.description: The dollar extended a decline, erasing its gains since the start of the war in the Middle East, after a report said the US and Iran are nearing an agreement to end the conflict.

item.title: Oil Slumps After Report US and Iran Near Deal to End War
item.description: Oil slumped for a second day after Axios reported that the US and Iran are getting close to a one-page memorandum of understanding to end the war.

item.title: Thailand Clears $29 Billion Investments Led by TikTok Project
item.description: Thailand approved 958 billion baht ($29 billion) of investment projects, led by TikTok System (Thailand) Co.’s planned expansion of data infrastructure as the country pushes to become a regional hub for artificial intelligence and cloud services.

item.title: Adnoc Appears to Export Second LNG Shipment Through Hormuz
item.description: Abu Dhabi National Oil Co. appears to have been able to export a second liquefied natural gas shipment through the Strait of Hormuz, according to ship-tracking data compiled by Bloomberg, even as the Iran war halts most traffic.

item.title: Global Bonds Rally as Oil Falls on Mounting Hopes for Peace Deal
item.description: Global bonds rallied as speculation about a potential peace deal between the US and Iran drove down energy prices, curbing bets on interest rate hikes as inflation expectations eased.

item.title: Nvidia Is Facing More Competition and It’s Spooking Investors
item.description: This earnings season has delivered plenty of good news for the artificial intelligence trade, but instead of bidding up Nvidia Corp. shares, investors have been dumping them.

item.title: UK Firms Impose Fuel Surcharges as Input Costs Soar, PMI Shows
item.description: UK businesses raised prices at the fastest pace in over three years, as they sought to pass on soaring raw material and energy costs to consumers despite a subdued economic backdrop.

item.title: Czech Inflation Quickens as Central Bank Gauges Rates Cushion
item.description: Czech inflation accelerated for a second month on more expensive fuels as the central bank is expected to maintain its wait-and-see mode on interest rates when policymakers meet this week.

item.title: Euro-Zone Wage Growth to Quicken in Second Half of This Year
item.description: Euro-area pay growth is set to accelerate in the second half of the year, the European Central Bank said as it gauges whether spillovers from high energy costs will require it to increase interest rates.

item.title: Standstill in India Gold Imports Drags On, Threatens Supply
item.description: Indian banks have been unable to import gold and silver for five weeks, an unusually long halt that’s pushing up domestic prices and threatening shortages in the world’s second-biggest bullion market.

item.title: Shipowners Grapple for Hormuz Fixes After Trump Drops Plan
item.description: The suspension of a short-lived US plan to guide vessels through the Strait of Hormuz has underscored the difficulty of securing the narrow waterway and left shipowners grappling for credible workarounds.

item.title: US Gasoline Hits $4.50, Nears All-Time High as Iran Fuel Crunch Grows
item.description: US gasoline prices topped $4.50 a gallon for the first time since July 2022, extending their march higher as drivers face further strain from the prolonged conflict in the Middle East.

item.title: Trader Trafigura Plans to Move Key Holding Company to Bermuda
item.description: Commodities trader Trafigura Group plans to re-domicile a key holding company through which employees own their shares in the business from the Netherlands to Bermuda.

item.title: Hyundai Robot Performs Handstand, Complex Gymnastics Moves
item.description: Hyundai Motor Co.’s robotics unit released a video showing its production-ready Atlas humanoid robot performing complex gymnastics, marking a first-of-its-kind live demonstration that could signal the machine is nearing commercialization.

item.title: Next Lifts Outlook as Strong Sales Offset Higher Iran Costs
item.description: Next Plc raised its profit and sales outlook as the British fashion and homewares retailer saw robust demand at the start of the year, even as estimated costs related to the conflict in the Middle East tripled.

item.title: Orsted Keeps Guidance as Europe Focus Drives Turnaround
item.description: Orsted A/S reaffirmed its earnings guidance for the year as the Danish utility doubles down on its European business.

item.title: Diageo Sales Gain Driven by Growth in Africa, Latin America
item.description: Diageo Plc’s sales unexpectedly rose as growth in Africa and Latin America was enough to offset significant weakness in the US.

item.title: China’s Chip Fund in Talks to Lead DeepSeek Funding, FT Says
item.description: China’s main chip-sector investment fund is in discussions to lead a fundraising round for DeepSeek at a valuation of about $45 billion, the Financial Times reported, citing people with knowledge of the talks.

item.title: Indonesia Hails End of ‘5% Growth Curse’ Despite Analyst Doubts
item.description: Indonesia’s finance minister said the economy’s strong start to the year is evidence the government’s strategies are working, but analysts warn that fading fiscal support and pressure on the rupiah will weigh on growth in the months ahead.

item.title: Vestas Beats Profit Estimates as Wind Turbine Orders Swell
item.description: Vestas Wind Systems A/S beat analysts estimates for profits in the first quarter of the year, as demand for wind turbines increased.

item.title: Infineon Forecast Beats as AI Boosts Power Chip Business
item.description: Infineon Technologies AG forecast revenue that beat analysts’ expectations in the current quarter as the German chipmaker benefits from a spending boom on artificial intelligence infrastructure.

item.title: Lufthansa Reports Narrower Loss, Sees Risks from Fuel Supplies
item.description: Deutsche Lufthansa AG posted a smaller-than-expected loss in the first quarter as strong demand for longhaul flights helped offset volatile fuel costs and disruption from labor strikes.

item.title: Romania Allows Currency to Weaken as Politics Fuel Selloff
item.description: A Romanian political crisis is once again testing the country’s managed float currency regime, with the central bank allowing the leu to slip to record lows this week.

item.title: Modi, Lam Meet as India and Vietnam Deepen AI, Defense Ties
item.description: India and Vietnam agreed to strengthen economic and defense ties as both sides seek to deepen their partnership while tensions persist in the Middle East.

item.title: Yen Rallies to Build on Gains Spurred by Recent Intervention
item.description: The yen rallied as much as 1.8% against the dollar, building upon gains triggered when Japanese officials intervened in markets to prop up their currency on April 30.

item.title: Sany Heavy Is Said to Consider IPO for Electric Unit in Hong Kong
item.description: Sany Heavy Industry Co. is considering an initial public offering for one of its units in Hong Kong, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Australian Pension Fund Tilts to US Over Local Stocks on AI Boom
item.description: Brighter Super, a A$37 billion ($26.8 billion) Australian pension fund, is tilting its portfolio away from the local share market in favor of global stocks as artificial intelligence continues to gain momentum in the US.

item.title: State Fund IPO Opens Door to Buy Into Uzbekistan’s Growth Story
item.description: Uzbekistan is kicking off a planned series of initial public offerings with the sale of about a 30% stake in the state investment fund, as part of the Central Asian nation’s push to entice international investors.
